comment: Single source of truth for ACCEPTED / known divergences from C Graphviz. Joined at report-time into PARITY.md (dashboard.ts) and the rules gate (rules-gate.ts) via accepted.ts — parity.json itself stays pure (verdict only). An accepted delta is a deliberate, documented, won't-fix difference; everything non-conformant NOT listed here is an unaccepted 'tracked' gap we intend to close. Adding/removing an entry must be paired with the prose in docs/known-divergences.md (the accepted-divergences.test.ts guard enforces that per-id parity entries are still non-conformant, so the list cannot rot silently). match: {id} exact | {idPattern} glob | {engineIn} engine list. scope: parity (dashboard) | rules (rules-gate) | both.

entries.bound: fine node coordinates; topology + ranks preserved, positions within a small epsilon
entries.reason: iterative force-model layouts depend on FMA / Math.pow rounding that is not bit-reproducible across JS engines and CPUs (same boundary as the Arm-pow port). Matches zero graphs in the all-dot corpus today; kept for completeness.

entries.bound: maxΔ~10pt on one flat-edge arc (376->76); bbox/nodes/labels conformant
entries.reason: symmetric-channel bezier-split tie broken by ~1e-14 noise; Apple macOS libm hypot is position-dependent (mirrors congruent arcs) and matches no portable hypot, so the port keeps a consistent translation-equivariant tie-break.

entries.bound: maxΔ 49 numeric only; identical element tree (both sides lose edge 3->16)
entries.reason: oracle lays out from an acknowledged-broken init_rank recovery state (aux-graph cycle; upstream xfail #2796, draft MR !4849, cf. #2471). The verify-oracle-bug-family mission fixed the port's stale-cluster-window defect (rec_reset_vlists, flat.c:333) making the x-aux inputs line-identical to C; the port now follows C through the recovery (same lost edge 3->16, 212/212). Residual numeric deltas are recovery-state NS behavior, deliberately not chased. Re-measure when upstream fixes #2796.

entries.bound: whole-layout numeric (maxΔ ~9063); element tree identical incl. the same 6 lost edges
entries.reason: oracle in the same acknowledged-broken init_rank recovery family (upstream xfail #2471, worst instance). Ranking + x-aux constraint inputs verified line-identical (253 rank2 calls) after the family fixes; both sides lose the identical 6 edges via C's triangulation debris. Residual numeric deltas are NS behavior from the infeasible/cyclic recovery state — not chased. Re-measure when upstream fixes #2471.

entries.bound: maxΔ 503 on pathplan-fallback spline debris; 11/11 edges on both sides; nodes/clusters conformant
entries.reason: acknowledged-broken pathplan input (upstream xfail #1435): both sides hit ear-clip triangulation dead ends and recover through warn-and-continue + straight-line fallbacks whose debris differs. All 16 NS constraint dumps line-identical; the port's fatal-triangulation misport was fixed (shortest.c:333 parity) so no edges are lost on either side. Re-measure when upstream fixes #1435.

entries.bound: maxΔ 0; element-tree difference only (the oracle's lost struct1->struct3)
entries.reason: the dev-build headless oracle loses struct1:f2->struct3:here to a Pshortestpath failure (ancient record-routing loss family #102/#242/#274/#1323); the port routes both edges from line-identical NS inputs and identical node geometry (maxΔ 0). Stable graphviz 15.0.0 also renders it — oracle-side regression, not a port gap.

node geometry 0.00; the dev-build headless oracle (15.1.0~dev) drops struct1:f2->struct3:here via a Pshortestpath regression. Stable graphviz 15.0.0 AND the port both render it (edge1 conformant to graphviz.org). Oracle-build regression, not a port bug.
node widths match headless exactly (rx 34.64); residual 1pt is node2's x under a :sw compass-port edge — a pre-existing x-NS/compass-port gap the LUT widths masked, not measurement.
